title: TeraWulf Shifts to AI Compute as Q1 Loss Widens, Bitcoin Falls
text: TeraWulf reported a Q1 2026 net loss of $427 million, significantly higher than prior quarters, but the company's strategic focus remains firmly on AI and HPC infrastructure expansion rather than Bitcoin mining optimization. The loss was substantially driven by a $216.3 million non-cash charge related to changes in warrant fair value. Despite the large reported loss, the company's operating fundamentals show the business model shifting positively with HPC revenue now exceeding mining revenue for the first time. The company's strategic position has strengthened with $3.1 billion in cash supporting aggressive capacity deployment. Analyst sentiment has turned decidedly positive, with multiple researchers raising price targets in May 2026 based on the successful proof of concept for long-term HPC contracts. The broader context shows TeraWulf is successfully executing its pivot away from cyclical Bitcoin mining toward stable, contracted AI compute services backed by investment-grade counterparties.
